channel.title: Projects on Ryan E. Wong
channel.description: Recent content in Projects on Ryan E. Wong

item.title: d-gen
item.description: Updated on 12/10/2020 d-gen is a project that I developed as part of the 2020 CDAC Summer Lab under the mentorship of Professor Raul Castro-Ferenandez. The purpose of d-gen is to synthetically generate relational databases that adhere to a user-defined schema for the purposes of query testing and optimization. Relational databases are databases which contain tables filled with columns and rows of information, which may or may not hold some sort of “relation” to each other.

item.title: Xtract Container Service (XCS)
item.description: The Xtract Container Service (XCS) is a service I developed as a research assistant at Globus Labs under the mentorship of Tyler Skluzacek. XCS is a service for building and storing containers (the computing kind, not the storage kind), and is part of the greater XtractHub project. Xtract’s main feature is its ability to process heterogeneous data in a scalable manner. To do so, it uses FuncX, which utilizes containers, allowing for the large-scale execution of various functions on the same computing device.
